channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Added to UN Sexual Violence Blacklist for First Time
item.description: The United Nations has added Israel to its blacklist of parties responsible for sexual violence in conflict zones, marking an unprecedented diplomatic blow for the country amid ongoing international scrutiny of its military operations.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Ebola Cases Nearly Double in DR Congo as WHO Chief Visits Outbreak Epicenter
item.description: The WHO Director-General arrived in Bunia as confirmed Ebola cases doubled within days, with the outbreak spreading amid insecurity and distrust. Aid cuts are hampering response efforts in the conflict-affected region.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Concludes Iran Meeting Without Announcing Deal
item.description: President Trump ended a highly anticipated meeting on potential Iran negotiations without announcing any decision, leaving the Middle East in suspense about the future of the ceasefire and potential military actions.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Soldiers Reach Nabatieh, One of Southern Lebanons Biggest Cities
item.description: Israeli ground forces have advanced into Nabatieh, one of the largest cities in southern Lebanon, marking a significant escalation in the Israel-Hezbollah conflict as military talks between the sides begin.

item.title: 🇲🇲🇮🇳 Myanmars Min Aung Hlaing Makes First Foreign Visit as Leader to India
item.description: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing embarked on his first foreign tour since seizing power, visiting India in a significant diplomatic move as the military government seeks international legitimacy.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Defense Secretary Hegseth Tones Down China Rhetoric at Singapore Forum
item.description: At the Shangri-La defense forum, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth assured Pacific allies of US commitment to the region while softening previous warnings about China, following Trump’s recent Beijing summit with Xi Jinping.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Military Blocks Sixth Ship in Iranian Port Blockade
item.description: A Gambia-flagged bulk carrier became the sixth vessel stopped by US forces attempting to breach the blockade of Iranian ports. The fragile ceasefire continues as nuclear negotiations proceed.

item.title: 🇵🇭🇨🇳 Philippines Warns of Ongoing China Threat Despite Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. stated the Philippines remains under “severe threat” from China despite recent US-China diplomatic thaw, highlighting ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 US Plans Accelerated Troop Withdrawal from Europe
item.description: The Pentagon plans to speed up the withdrawal of US troops from European bases and will present proposals to NATO allies next month, according to German newspaper Welt am Sonntag.

item.title: ⛽🌍 IMF, World Bank, IEA Chiefs Warn of Summer Fuel Scarcity from Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Heads of major international economic institutions warned that prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could cause severe fuel shortages during peak summer demand, straining global energy supplies.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇺 US General holds rare meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay
item.description: The head of US Southern Command met with Cuban military officials for the first time in recent memory amid growing concerns in Cuba about possible US military action.

item.title: 🇨🇳⚔️ China creates autonomous drone warfare algorithm for jamming environments
item.description: Chinese scientists developed a smart web algorithm that identifies objects as friend, foe or terrain, enabling drone swarms to operate when communication is jammed and vision is blocked.

item.title: 🌐🔌 17 nations launch pact to protect undersea cables without US and China
item.description: Singapore announced a new pact with 17 nations to collaborate on protecting vital undersea cables, with both the US and China notably absent from the agreement.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US softens tone on China at Shangri-La Dialogue after Xi-Trump summit
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth avoided directly describing Beijing as a threat at the regional security forum, in contrast to previous years, following the recent Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇬🇧🇦🇺 US, UK and Australia to Develop Underwater Drone Technology Under AUKUS Pact
item.description: The three nations announced plans to jointly develop underwater drone technology to protect undersea cables and boost naval defense capabilities. The initiative is part of the AUKUS military partnership aimed at countering China's growing influence in the Indo-Pacific region.

item.title: 🇧🇷🇺🇸 Lula Slams US Designation of Brazilian Gangs as Terrorist Organizations
item.description: Brazil's President Lula da Silva said Brazil will not be treated as a 'tinpot country' after the US designated the PCC and Red Command cartels as foreign terrorist organizations. The move is seen as a boost for Lula's far-right challenger Flavio Bolsonaro in October's presidential election.

item.title: 🇭🇺🇪🇺 EU Set to Release 16 Billion Euros to Hungary After Orban's Defeat
item.description: The European Union is preparing to release 16 billion euros in previously withheld funds to Hungary after the defeat of Viktor Orban's government. The funds were frozen due to rule of law and anti-corruption concerns, and will now be released as Hungary enacts required reforms.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Mexico Senate Passes Bill Allowing Election Annulment for Foreign Interference
item.description: Mexico's senate approved a constitutional amendment that would allow election results to be annulled for 'foreign interference,' defined to include illicit financing, propaganda, misinformation and digital manipulation. Critics say the bill could give the ruling party power to overturn opposition victories.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iranian Hard-Liners Mobilize to Derail Potential US Peace Deal
item.description: A political fight is unfolding in Iran as hard-liners use rallies, state media and public statements to undermine negotiations with the US. The faction opposes concessions to Washington as Trump deliberates on a framework agreement to end the conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Italy cancels Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
item.description: Italian authorities banned two concerts in Reggio Emilia due to public order concerns and potential protests. West has faced cancellations across Europe following antisemitic remarks, while Scott has been under scrutiny since the Astroworld crowd crush that killed 10 people.

item.title: 🇦🇫 Truck crash kills 22 Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan
item.description: A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan overturned in eastern Afghanistan, killing at least 22 people including 10 children. The crash comes as millions of Afghans have returned from Pakistan and Iran following crackdowns on migrants.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli troops push deeper into Lebanon as Pentagon talks begin
item.description: Israeli forces entered the southern Lebanese village of Dibbine near Marjayoun as Lebanese and Israeli military officials held their first direct talks at the Pentagon in decades. The violence continues despite a nominal ceasefire that began in April.

item.title: 🇪🇸 Spain legalizes over 500,000 undocumented immigrants in regularization program
item.description: Spain is processing over 549,000 applications to regularize undocumented immigrants by June 30, a stark contrast to US deportation policies. Prime Minister Sánchez called it an act of normalization for people already part of Spanish daily life.

item.title: 🇷🇴 NATO Condemns Russia After Drone Hits Romanian Residential Building
item.description: A Russian drone likely hit over Ukraine by air defenses crashed into a residential block in Romania, marking a significant escalation as Russian attacks on NATO territory continue. NATO and EU both condemned the incident.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Defense Secretary Hegseth: US Not Turning Back on Asia Allies, But Expects Defense Boost
item.description: At a Singapore security summit, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reaffirmed US commitment to Asian allies while pressing them to increase their own defense spending and arms purchases.
